_metadata.description: Seed data for the sub_groups reference table

data.name: Dinobots
data.notes: Autobot sub-team with dinosaur alt modes. Created by Wheeljack and Ratchet

data.name: Minibots
data.notes: Smaller Autobot warriors. Includes Bumblebee, Cliffjumper, Brawn, etc.

data.name: Aerialbots
data.notes: Autobot air team. Combine to form Superion. Created via Vector Sigma

data.name: Protectobots
data.notes: Autobot emergency services team. Combine to form Defensor

data.name: Technobots
data.notes: Autobot scientist team. Combine to form Computron. Created by Grimlock. Introduced Season 3

data.name: Throttlebots
data.notes: Autobot speed team. Includes Goldbug, Chase, Freeway, etc. Introduced Season 3

data.name: Autobot Clones
data.notes: Fastlane and Cloudraker. Identical robot modes, different alt modes. Introduced Season 4

data.name: Headmasters
data.notes: Transformers who binary-bond with head partners. Both factions. In NA: Nebulan partners. In JP: small Cybertronians from planet Master

data.name: Targetmasters
data.notes: Transformers who binary-bond with weapon partners. Both factions. Introduced Season 4 (NA) / Headmasters E27 (JP)

data.name: Seekers
data.notes: Decepticon jet warriors. Original trio: Starscream, Skywarp, Thundercracker. Includes Coneheads and retroactively named background jets

data.name: Constructicons
data.notes: Decepticon construction vehicle team. Combine to form Devastator. First combiner team

data.name: Insecticons
data.notes: Decepticon insect warriors. Bombshell, Shrapnel, Kickback. Can clone themselves. See also: Deluxe Insecticons

data.name: Stunticons
data.notes: Decepticon car team. Combine to form Menasor. Created via Vector Sigma

data.name: Combaticons
data.notes: Decepticon military vehicle team. Combine to form Bruticus. Created by Starscream

data.name: Triple Changers
data.notes: Transformers with two alt modes. Decepticons (Blitzwing, Astrotrain) and Autobots (Sandstorm, Broadside, Springer)

data.name: Predacons
data.notes: Decepticon animal team. Combine to form Predaking. Introduced Season 3

data.name: Terrorcons
data.notes: Decepticon monster team. Combine to form Abominus. Introduced Season 3

data.name: Seacons
data.notes: Decepticon sea creature team. Combine to form Piranacon/King Poseidon. 6 members (Nautilator becomes weapon)

data.name: Decepticon Clones
data.notes: Pounce and Wingspan. Identical robot modes, different alt modes. Introduced Season 4

data.name: Duocons
data.notes: Two vehicles that combine into one robot. Battletrap and Flywheels. Introduced Season 4

data.name: Horrorcons
data.notes: Headmaster triple-changer Decepticons. Apeface and Snapdragon. Introduced Season 4

data.name: Cassettes
data.notes: Microcassette Transformers. Autobot (Blaster's) and Decepticon (Soundwave/Soundblaster's). Includes combining cassettes

data.name: Battlechargers
data.notes: Runabout and Runamuck. Pull-back auto-transforming. Introduced Season 3

data.name: Trainbots
data.notes: Japanese-exclusive Autobot combiner team. Six train alt modes → Raiden. Only 6-member Autobot combiner in G1. JP Headmasters

data.name: Monsterbots
data.notes: Autobot beast warriors. Grotusque, Doublecross, Repugnus. Called Firebots in Japan. JP Headmasters / 1987 US toy

data.name: Pretenders
data.notes: Transformers with organic or armored outer shells. Both factions. In JP Masterforce: Autobots shrink to human size; Decepticons become monsters. 1988-1989 US toyline

data.name: Godmasters
data.notes: JP Masterforce: Humans who merge with transtector robot bodies via engine bonding. Western equivalent: Powermasters. Both factions

data.name: Headmaster Juniors
data.notes: JP Masterforce: Human children/teens who become Headmasters via Masterbraces. Both factions. Autobot: Shuta Go, Cab, Minerva. Decepticon: Wilder, Cancer, Bullhorn

data.name: Brainmasters
data.notes: JP Victory: Autobots with small partners that form the host's face. Star Saber, Blacker, Braver, Laster. Combine to form Road Caesar (minus Star Saber)

data.name: Multiforce
data.notes: JP Victory: Six Autobots that can pair-combine with any member, or all six form Landcross. Unique pair-combining gimmick

data.name: Breastforce
data.notes: JP Victory: Elite Decepticon team. Each has a breast animal partner that becomes a weapon. Six members combine to form Liokaiser

data.name: Dinoforce
data.notes: JP Victory: Decepticon Pretenders with dinosaur shells. Six members combine to form Dinoking. Bumbling comic relief. Recolored Pretender Monsters

data.name: Rescue Patrol
data.notes: JP Victory: Autobot Micromaster team. Holi (leader), Fire, Boater, Pipo. Child-like personalities

data.name: Powered Masters
data.notes: JP Zone: Autobot leaders with motorized gimmicks and base modes. Dai Atlas, Sonic Bomber, Roadfire. Combine to form Sky Powered

data.name: Omnibots
data.notes: 1985 mail-order Autobots. Camshaft, Downshift, Overdrive. Triple changers (car/armed car/robot). Diaclone Double Changer molds

data.name: Powerdashers
data.notes: 1985 mail-order Autobots. Pull-back motor gimmick. Aragon, Cromar, Zetar. Names unofficial (from Diaclone)

data.name: Deluxe Insecticons
data.notes: 1985 Decepticons. Barrage, Chop Shop, Ransack, Venom. Bandai molds — excluded from cartoon due to Takara licensing conflict

data.name: Deluxe Vehicles
data.notes: 1985 Autobots. Roadbuster, Whirl. Takatoku/Dorvack molds — excluded from cartoon. Prominent Wreckers in Marvel UK comics

data.name: Jumpstarters
data.notes: 1985 Autobots. Topspin, Twin Twist. Pull-back auto-transform gimmick. Animation models exist but never used. Wreckers in comics

data.name: Triggerbots
data.notes: 1988 Autobots. Backstreet, Dogfight, Override. Flip-out gun gimmick. Budget price point

data.name: Triggercons
data.notes: 1988 Decepticons. Crankcase, Ruckus, Windsweeper. Flip-out gun gimmick. Decepticon counterpart to Triggerbots

data.name: Sparkabots
data.notes: 1988 Autobots. Fizzle, Guzzle, Sizzle. Friction-spark gimmick. Called Sparkler Mini-Bots on packaging

data.name: Firecons
data.notes: 1988 Decepticons. Cindersaur, Flamefeather, Sparkstalker. Friction-spark gimmick. Brief cameo in JP Masterforce (non-speaking)

data.name: Powermasters
data.notes: 1988 Hasbro. Nebulan engine partners unlock transformation. Both factions. JP equivalent: Godmasters (different characters). Includes Powermaster Optimus Prime

data.name: Double Targetmasters
data.notes: 1988 Hasbro. Transformers with TWO Nebulan weapon partners each. Both factions. Landfill, Quickmix, Scoop (A), Needlenose, Quake, Spinister (D)

data.name: Pretender Beasts
data.notes: 1988 Hasbro. Pretenders with beast shells. Catilla, Chainclaw (A), Carnivac, Snarler (D). Carnivac is a fan-favorite Wrecker from Marvel UK

data.name: Pretender Vehicles
data.notes: 1988 Hasbro. Pretenders whose shells are driveable vehicles. Gunrunner (A), Roadgrabber (D)

data.name: Classic Pretenders
data.notes: 1989 Hasbro. Simplified versions of classic characters (Bumblebee, Grimlock, Jazz, Starscream) in Pretender shells. Also sold shell-less as Legends (Kmart exclusive)

data.name: Mega Pretenders
data.notes: 1989 Hasbro. Pretenders whose vehicle-mode shells also transform. Vroom, Crossblades (A), Thunderwing (D). Thunderwing is a major Marvel comics character

data.name: Ultra Pretenders
data.notes: 1989 Hasbro. Both inner robot AND outer shell transform independently. Skyhammer (A), Roadblock (D). Extremely prone to gold plastic syndrome

data.name: Pretender Monsters
data.notes: 1989 Decepticons. Six small Pretenders that combine to form Monstructor. JP counterpart: Dinoking (Victory, recolored with dinosaur shells). Very prone to GPS

data.name: Titans
data.notes: Giant city/base-forming Transformers. Transform into cities, battle stations, or planetary-scale bases. Includes G1 city-bots and Japanese-exclusive base-formers.

data.name: Fuzors
data.notes: Beast Wars Transformers whose beast modes are hybrids of two different animals. Introduced in Season 2. Includes Silverbolt (wolf/eagle), Quickstrike (scorpion/cobra), and toy-only characters

data.name: Transmetals
data.notes: Beast Wars characters upgraded by the quantum surge into metallic beast modes with vehicle capabilities. Introduced in Season 2. Includes Transmetal, Transmetal 2, and Optimal forms

data.name: Vehicon Generals
data.notes: Beast Machines: Megatron's elite Vehicon commanders. Each controls a type of drone army. Tankor (tanks), Jetstorm (aero-drones), Thrust (cycle-drones), Obsidian (copter-drones), Strika (assault vehicles)

data.name: Autobot Brothers
data.notes: RiD 2001 trio of Autobot car warriors: Side Burn, Prowl, and X-Brawn. Earth-based patrol team

data.name: Team Bullet Train
data.notes: RiD 2001 Autobot trio that patrols Earth's railways. Railspike, Rapid Run, and Midnight Express combine to form Rail Racer

data.name: Build Team
data.notes: RiD 2001 Autobot construction vehicle team. Wedge (leader), Grimlock, Heavy Load, and Hightower combine to form Landfill

data.name: Spychangers
data.notes: RiD 2001 Autobot special ops team of six smaller-than-average bots. Led by Hot Shot. Specialize in stealth and covert espionage

data.name: Predacon Trio
data.notes: RiD 2001 trio of bumbling Predacon minions under Sky-Byte's command: Dark Scream, Gas Skunk, and Slapper. Beast mode Transformers

data.name: Commandos
data.notes: RiD 2001 Decepticon military team led by Scourge. Mega-Octane, Ro-Tor, Movor, Armorhide, and Rollbar combine to form Ruination. Brainwashed Autobot protoforms. Also known as Combatrons

data.name: Tiger Force
data.notes: Elite G.I. Joe jungle warfare unit with distinctive tiger-stripe camouflage. 1988 sub-line featuring repainted classic characters and vehicles

data.name: Night Force
data.notes: Covert G.I. Joe nighttime operations team with dark color schemes. 1989 Toys 'R' Us exclusive sub-line. Largest ARAH sub-team by member count

data.name: Slaughter's Marauders
data.notes: Sgt. Slaughter's hand-picked elite team. 1989 sub-line with bright multi-color camouflage. Named after real-life WWF wrestler Sgt. Slaughter

data.name: Sky Patrol
data.notes: G.I. Joe airborne operations team equipped with working parachute packs. 1990 sub-line. Led by Sky Dive

data.name: Eco-Warriors
data.notes: Environmental combat team with color-change water-activated gimmick. 1991 sub-line. Includes both G.I. Joe and Cobra members

data.name: DEF
data.notes: Drug Elimination Force. G.I. Joe law enforcement-themed unit. 1992 sub-line featuring Bullet-Proof, Mutt, Cutter, and Shockwave vs Headman and Headhunter

data.name: Mega Marines
data.notes: G.I. Joe monster-fighting team equipped with moldable Bio-Armor. 1993 sub-line. Counterpart to Cobra's Mega Monsters

data.name: Star Brigade
data.notes: Space operations team for orbital and extraterrestrial missions. 1993-1994 sub-line. Includes both G.I. Joe and Cobra members. Final ARAH sub-line

data.name: Battle Force 2000
data.notes: Futuristic vehicle specialists with combining vehicles. 1987 sub-line. Members: Avalanche, Blaster, Blocker, Dee-Jay, Dodger, Knockdown, Maverick

data.name: Steel Brigade
data.notes: Mail-order custom G.I. Joe trooper. Members could personalize name and military specialty on included file card. 1987. Iconic gold-visored helmet

data.name: Dreadnoks
data.notes: Biker mercenary gang led by Zartan. Known for crude, destructive tendencies and love of grape soda and chocolate-covered doughnuts. Core members: Buzzer, Ripper, Torch

data.name: Crimson Guard
data.notes: Elite Cobra troopers known as 'Siegies' (from CG — Crimson Guard). Highly trained infiltrators posing as professionals in civilian society. Led by Crimson Guard Commanders Tomax and Xamot

data.name: Python Patrol
data.notes: Cobra stealth unit with radar-defeating Python camouflage process. 1989 sub-line featuring repainted Cobra vehicles and troopers in yellow and black

data.name: Headhunters
data.notes: Drug trafficking enforcers working for Headman. 1993 sub-line tied to DEF storyline. Cobra-allied organized crime

data.name: B.A.T.s
data.notes: Battle Android Troopers. Cobra's expendable robotic soldiers. First released 1986. Distinctive exposed chest circuitry. Multiple versions across ARAH and modern lines

data.name: Night Creepers
data.notes: Techno-ninja mercenaries hired by Cobra for espionage and assassination. First released 1990. Blend high-tech gadgetry with traditional ninja skills

data.name: Mega Monsters
data.notes: Cobra's mutant monster soldiers. 1993 sub-line counterpart to the Mega Marines. Bio-engineered creatures with moldable monster armor

data.name: Iron Grenadiers
data.notes: Destro's personal military force. Independent arms-dealing army with signature gold and black livery. Operates autonomously from Cobra. First released 1988

data.name: Ninja Force
data.notes: Ninja-trained operatives from both G.I. Joe and Cobra. 1992-1993 sub-line with spring-loaded action gimmick. Includes Snake Eyes, Storm Shadow, Scarlett, Slice, Dice

data.name: Shadow Ninjas
data.notes: Successor to Ninja Force with color-change and neon gimmicks. 1994 sub-line. Both G.I. Joe and Cobra members. Final ninja-themed ARAH sub-line

data.name: Street Action Mini-Con Team
data.notes: Armada Mini-Con team: High Wire, Grindor, Sureshock. Combine to form Perceptor. First Mini-Cons found on Earth

data.name: Air Defense Mini-Con Team
data.notes: Armada Mini-Con team: Jetstorm, Runway, Sonar. Combine to form the Star Saber sword, possibly the mightiest weapon known to Transformer-kind

data.name: Race Mini-Con Team
data.notes: Armada Mini-Con team: Dirt Boss, Downshift, Mirage. Combine to form the Skyboom Shield, the ultimate defensive force

data.name: Space Mini-Con Team
data.notes: Armada Mini-Con team: Astroscope, Payload, Sky Blast. Combine to form the Requiem Blaster, drawing on supernova energy

data.name: Omnicons
data.notes: Energon: Autobot-allied workers who mine and process raw energon into weapons and energon stars. Includes Arcee, Signal Flare, Skyblast, Strongarm

data.name: Terrorcons (Energon)
data.notes: Energon: Energon-devouring creatures created by Alpha Q. Often mass-produced drones. Led by Scorponok. Distinct from G1 Terrorcons

data.name: Construction Team
data.notes: Energon: Five Decepticon construction vehicles (Steamhammer, Bonecrusher, Duststorm, Sledge, Wideload) that combine to form Constructicon Maximus

data.name: Destruction Team
data.notes: Energon: Five super-energon-powered Decepticon military vehicles (Barricade, Blackout, Blight, Kickback, Stormcloud) that combine to form Bruticus Maximus

data.name: Air Team (Energon)
data.notes: Energon: Five Autobot jets (Storm Jet, Sky Shadow, Terradive, Treadshot, Windrazor) that combine to form Superion Maximus

data.name: Recon Mini-Con Team
data.notes: Cybertron Mini-Con trio (Jolt, Reverb, Six-Speed) from Gigantion who assist Vector Prime and the Autobots in reconnaissance

data.name: Wreckers (Movie)
data.notes: Movieverse Autobot combat engineers with NASCAR stock car alt modes. Leadfoot, Topspin, Roadbuster. Dark of the Moon (2011)

data.name: Guardian Knights
data.notes: Twelve ancient Cybertronian knights who combine to form Dragonstorm. The Last Knight (2017)

data.name: Mini Dinobots
data.notes: Baby Dinobot copies at Cade Yeager's junkyard. Sharp-T, Pterry, Tops. The Last Knight (2017)

data.name: Terrorcons (ROTB)
data.notes: Movieverse Terrorcons serving Unicron in Rise of the Beasts (2023). Led by Scourge. Includes Nightbird and Battletrap

data.name: Elite Guard
data.notes: Animated: Cybertron's premier military force. Led by Ultra Magnus. Includes Sentinel Prime, Jazz, Blurr, Jetfire, Jetstorm, Warpath

data.name: Team Athenia
data.notes: Animated: Autobot unit led by Rodimus Prime. Members: Ironhide, Hot Shot, Brawn, Red Alert. Encountered Lockdown during a mission

data.name: Starscream Clones
data.notes: Animated: Clones of Starscream, each embodying one aspect of his personality. Skywarp (cowardice), Thundercracker (ego), Sunstorm (sycophancy), Ramjet (lies), Slipstream (unknown trait)

data.name: Team Chaar
data.notes: Animated: Decepticon unit based on planet Chaar. Led by Strika. Members: Oil Slick, Blackout, Spittor, Cyclonus

data.name: Team Prime
data.notes: Core Autobot team on Earth in Transformers: Prime. Optimus Prime, Bumblebee, Arcee, Bulkhead, Ratchet, Cliffjumper, Wheeljack, Smokescreen, Ultra Magnus

data.name: Wreckers (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Wreckers. Elite Autobot commando unit. Includes Bulkhead and Wheeljack in Prime

data.name: Seekers (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Decepticon jet warriors. Starscream, Thundercracker, Skywarp, Dreadwing, Skyquake, Slipstream

data.name: Predacons (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Predacons. Ancient Cybertronians cloned by Shockwave from fossilized CNA. Predaking, Darksteel, Skylynx

data.name: Insecticons (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Insecticons. Hive-minded swarm of Decepticon warriors. Hardshell is the only named individual

data.name: Dinobots (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Dinobots (Lightning Strike Coalition). Experimented on by Shockwave in FOC. Grimlock, Swoop, Slug, Snarl

data.name: Combaticons (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Combaticons. Combine to form Bruticus. Onslaught, Blast Off, Brawl, Swindle, Vortex. Featured in FOC

data.name: Aerialbots (Aligned)
data.notes: Aligned continuity Aerialbots. Autobot air team in WFC. Jetfire, Air Raid, Silverbolt

data.name: Rescue Bots
data.notes: Aligned continuity Rescue Bots team stationed in Griffin Rock. Heatwave, Chase, Blades, Boulder, plus later additions

data.name: Rescue Bots Academy
data.notes: Training academy for new Rescue Bot recruits. Hot Shot, Whirl, Hoist, Medix, Wedge

data.name: Burns Family
data.notes: Griffin Rock first responder family who partner with the Rescue Bots. Chief Charlie, Kade, Dani, Graham, Cody Burns

data.name: Stunticons (RiD)
data.notes: RiD 2015 Decepticon combiner team. Motormaster, Heatseeker, Wildbreak, Dragstrip, Slashmark combine to form Menasor
